common_leave_description: This link goes to a site docolin doesn't control. Continue only if you trust it.
browse_meta_description: Explore docolin by topic, from operating systems, hardware, and networking to security, programming, and beyond.
browse_subtitle: What the community is verifying, discussing, and publishing right now.
browse_trending_window_week: The most verification and discussion activity this week.
browse_trending_window_month: The most verification and discussion activity this month.
browse_trending_window_all: The most verified docos so far.
browse_look_subtitle_personal: Matched to the systems you read about and run.
browse_topics_subtitle: Know what you need? Go straight to the shelf.
browse_hero_pango_one: Pango rates this {score} after one confirmation
browse_look_whisper: Inferred from your reading on this device. Never sent anywhere.
about_meta_description: docolin is the open commons for technical knowledge: every guide signed-verified on a real system, every version preserved, and humans and AI reading the same source. AGPL, free forever.
about_hero_title: Documentation as a commons, not a product.
about_hero_subtitle: docolin is an open platform where every guide carries a signed record that it worked on a real system, every version is kept, and humans and AI agents read from the same source. AGPL-licensed, free forever, funded by sponsors instead of by selling you or your data.
about_hero_cta_mcp: See it through an AI agent
about_thesis_title: Technical knowledge is fragmented, stale, and going invisible.
about_thesis_body: The working knowledge that used to live in forum threads, wikis, and blog posts is scattered, undated, and increasingly absorbed into AI answers with no link back and no way to tell whether it still works. The market has split in two, and both halves are failing the reader.
about_thesis_machine_body: The tools that feed AI agents say outright that they can't guarantee what they serve is correct, and their pipelines are closed.
about_thesis_human_title: Human-facing docs that are dying or scraped
about_thesis_human_body: Forums decay, great wikis gatekeep, and the answers people wrote are licensed away or pulled into models with no credit.
about_thesis_close: docolin sits in the middle that neither half can occupy: current, verified, attributed, and open at the same time.
about_different_title: Built on things nothing else has.
about_different_verify_body: Every guide carries signed records that it worked on a specific system and version, left by a real person or a named agent. Proof it works, not a popularity count.
about_different_open_body: AGPL-licensed, so it can't be bought and locked down. Anyone can fork it, but the license forces every fork to stay just as open: the same platform and content, free for everyone.
about_different_attribution_body: When an AI agent uses a guide, your name rides into the citation by design. Credit survives the machine.
about_different_resolution_title: The right version for your setup
about_different_resolution_body: Version is a retrieval key, not a guess: a Fedora 42 query never returns a Fedora 41 page, and one soft link sends an Ubuntu reader to UFW and a Fedora reader to firewalld.
about_open_title: Open by construction, so it can't be taken away.
about_open_body: docolin's platform code is AGPL-licensed. It can't be bought and locked down, it outlives its maintainer, and the license forces anyone who runs it to share their source. Content stays under attribution licenses; a company reading docolin through MCP doesn't inherit AGPL, it just has to keep your name on your work. This is the structural answer to what happened when a Q&A giant licensed its contributors' answers to an AI lab: docolin is constitutionally unable to do that to you.
about_open_governance: Anyone can open a pull request or a discussion. Merges stay with the author and their invited maintainers, and abandoned guides can be forked. Admins, orgs, projects, docos: GitHub, but for documentation.
about_wedge_title: Launching with Linux. Built for everything.
about_wedge_body: Windows 10 reached end of life in October 2025, sending the biggest wave of mainstream Linux newcomers in over a decade toward help that either scares them off or can't be trusted. Linux is a focused audience whose pain the design answers directly: a structured taxonomy for the sprawl, per-setup filtering for distro and version fragmentation, and verified-working stamps that separate advice that works on your machine from advice that worked once for someone in 2019. None of the architecture is Linux-specific. It's where docolin starts, not where it ends.
about_not_additive: A replacement for Wikipedia, ArchWiki, or Stack Overflow. Their content is valuable; docolin is additive.
about_not_competitor: A competitor to Mintlify, GitBook, or Docusaurus. Keep your docs site and mirror community content to docolin.
about_not_training: An AI training company. docolin grounds AI; it doesn't sell training data.
about_pango_body: docolin's mascot is a pangolin: overlapping scales like a structured knowledge tree, and it curls up to defend itself, like docs that actually protect the people reading them. Pango also rates your docos, a warm score from 0 to 1000 for how well a guide has held up on real systems.
about_involved_title: Built in the open. Come help.
about_involved_body: docolin is pre-alpha and built in public under AGPL. The fastest ways to help: read a guide and stamp whether it worked, create an account and host your own, mirror your project's docs, or sponsor the infrastructure.
projects_meta_description: Mirror your project's docs to docolin and keep your repo, your pull requests, and merge control. Get community contributions, verification, version history, and attribution on every AI citation. AGPL, free forever.
projects_hero_title: A home for your docs that credits you.
projects_hero_subtitle: Your README is scraped by a dozen models and surfaced with no link back, and your best answers elsewhere were licensed off to an AI lab. Point docolin at your repo and get a canonical, attributed home: community contributions and verification on top, while your issues, pull requests, and merge rights stay exactly where they are.
projects_how_title: Bring docs however you keep them.
projects_how_git_body: Point docolin at any repo. It syncs on every push, and your issues and pull requests stay in the GitHub workflow you already use.
projects_how_import_body: Bring MkDocs, Mintlify, or Docusaurus docs in one pass. Plain content imports cleanly; platform-specific components are mapped where they can be and flagged where they can't.
projects_how_editor_body: An in-app editor is on the way for authors who don't live in Git. No repo required.
projects_keep_control_body: Anyone can open a pull request or a discussion. Merging stays with you and your invited maintainers; lock a branch and the community can still propose, just not overwrite.
projects_keep_attribution_body: Your name rides into every citation, including when an AI agent serves your guide through MCP. Credit by construction, not by courtesy.
projects_keep_history_body: Each version stays addressable forever with its own verification record. A guide that worked on 22.04 doesn't vanish when 24.04 lands.
projects_cta_title: Give your docs a home that can't be taken away.
projects_cta_body: docolin's platform is AGPL, so it can't be bought and locked down, and a company reading your docs through MCP doesn't inherit AGPL, it just has to keep your name on your work. Free forever, funded by sponsors.
sponsor_meta_description: docolin is free forever: no paid tier, nothing to sell about you. It's funded by infrastructure sponsors whose tooling benefits from the substrate AI grounds against, and sponsorship buys no influence over content.
sponsor_hero_title: Free forever, and honest about why.
sponsor_hero_subtitle: docolin has no paid tier, no private docs, and nothing to sell about you. It runs on lean infrastructure and is funded by sponsors, so the question every reader should ask, what's the catch, has a straight answer.
sponsor_model_title: Sponsorship, not user fees or data deals.
sponsor_model_body: The money comes from infrastructure companies, not from charging readers or contributors, and not from licensing anyone's work to AI labs. That's a deliberate contrast to the platforms that paywalled their docs and the Q&A site that sold its contributors' answers. The same platform and the same content are free for a solo writer and a Fortune 500 alike.
sponsor_guardrail_body: Sponsors get visible thanks and our gratitude. They do not get ranking influence, editorial say, special verification, or a back door to the data. The trust signals only mean something if they can't be bought, so they aren't for sale.
sponsor_who_title: Infrastructure that wins when the commons does.
sponsor_who_body: If your tooling benefits from being part of the substrate AI agents ground against, databases, hosting, search, developer tools, then sponsoring docolin keeps that substrate open and growing. If that sounds like you, we'd love to talk.
sponsor_cta_title: Want to keep the commons open?
sponsor_cta_body: Reach out and we'll find a fit that works. No pitch decks required.
compare_meta_description: An honest look at where docolin sits among AI-grounding tools, documentation platforms, and community wikis: what it does that none of them do, and where it's still behind.
compare_hero_subtitle: docolin isn't trying to beat documentation platforms at polish or out-archive Stack Overflow. It occupies a spot none of them can: verified, current, attributed, and open at once. Here's the honest version, including what it doesn't have yet.
compare_only_title: Four things the rest of the field doesn't.
compare_only_verify_body: A structured, signed record that a guide worked on a specific system and version, left by a real person or a named agent. Others have upvotes, edit dates, or freshness timestamps; none assert that the steps actually produced the result.
compare_only_open_body: AGPL-licensed and content-agnostic. It can't be acquired and locked down, and it isn't shaped only for developer docs, library snippets, or one company's product.
compare_only_attribution_body: Every snippet an agent serves carries the author's name by design, the thing the Stack Overflow data deals trampled.
compare_only_version_body: A query for one version never returns another's page, and every version stays addressable with its own verification record, not collapsed into a single rolling article.
compare_cat_ai_body: Tools like Context7 and DeepWiki feed agents, but one disclaims the accuracy of what it serves and the other derives wikis from source code. Neither carries human verification, and their backends are closed or auto-generated.
compare_cat_platforms_body: Mintlify, GitBook, and the rest are polished, but each company's docs are a private island with its own endpoint. There's no shared commons across projects and no cross-source verification standard.
compare_cat_community_body: Stack Overflow and ArchWiki hold real knowledge, but answers go stale with no signal, contributors lose attribution to scrapers, and there's no per-setup filtering or cross-source discovery.
compare_lack_body: Honesty cuts both ways. docolin doesn't have fifteen years of accumulated content or organic search authority, the polish and budget of a funded platform, or the existing client integrations and mindshare of the incumbents. The bet is that verification, open governance, and a shared commons become a stronger foundation than content inertia, the way Wikipedia overtook Encarta from a standing start.
terms_meta_description: The terms for using docolin: acceptable use, how content is licensed and attributed, and the no-warranty basis of community verification.
terms_use_body: docolin is an open, public commons. Use it to read, write, verify, and discuss technical knowledge in good faith. Don't post unlawful content, abuse other people, try to break or overload the platform, or misrepresent verification. Accounts that do may be limited or removed.
terms_content_body: Everything published to docolin is public, including content mirrored from private repositories. There is no private or paywalled content. Don't publish anything you need to keep confidential.
terms_license_body: You keep ownership of what you write. By publishing, you grant docolin and its readers, human and AI, the right to display, distribute, and cite your content with attribution. The platform code is licensed AGPL-3.0; a company reading docolin through MCP does not inherit AGPL, but must preserve attribution. Imported or mirrored content stays under its original license.
terms_verification_body: Verification stamps and Pango scores are signed reports from real users and agents about what worked on their systems. They are evidence, not a guarantee. docolin is provided as is, without warranty; always use your own judgment before running commands on your machine.
terms_changes_body: docolin is pre-alpha and evolving, so these terms will change. Material changes will be noted here with an updated date.
home_meta_title: docolin, structured knowledge for everything, built for humans and AI
home_meta_description: Open, community-verified documentation platform with attribution baked into every AI citation. Pre-alpha, AGPL, free forever. Launching with Linux help for newcomers.
home_hero_subtitle: Every guide on docolin carries a signed stamp showing it worked, on a real system, on a real date. The open commons for technical knowledge, read by humans and AI.
home_what_structured_body: Every guide lives under a kind path. The same link resolves differently depending on your setup, hardware, and version.
home_what_verified_body: A guide's Pango Score is a signed record that it worked, on real systems and versions, by humans and agents. Proof, not just popularity.
home_what_attributed_body: Your work stays credited even when an AI surfaces it. Contributor attribution baked into every citation, human or machine.
home_what_attributed_question: How do I install NVIDIA drivers on Ubuntu 24.04?
home_what_attributed_answer: On Ubuntu 24.04 with an RTX-series GPU, the recommended driver is the proprietary 550 series. Run sudo ubuntu-drivers install...
home_search_title: Ask once. Get the right answer for your setup.
home_search_body: Same docolin link. The result resolves to the guide that applies to your distro, hardware, and version. Verified by readers on similar systems.
home_search_right_title: AMDGPU on Fedora, out of the box
home_personas_readers_body: Find answers that match your setup. Verified by people running what you're running. No more 2019 blog posts dressed up as 2026 advice.
home_personas_contributors_body: Write once, get cited forever. Attribution travels with your work, even when AI tools surface it. The feedback loop that motivated good writing, restored.
home_personas_projects_body: Connect your repo on docolin. Auto-sync on push. Keep editorial control through branch locking. Community contributions, version history, search, and MCP grounding come for free.
home_personas_ai_body: One grounding surface that resolves across every source to the reader's exact setup, with the same verification standard everywhere. Every result an agent serves carries author attribution by construction.
home_personas_readers_mock_title: Install proprietary NVIDIA drivers on Ubuntu 24.04
home_personas_readers_mock_snippet: On Ubuntu 24.04 the recommended driver for RTX-series cards is the proprietary 550 series. The simplest path is...
home_personas_contributors_mock_citation: cited 47 times by AI assistants this week
home_why_title: The knowledge layer the web forgot to build for the AI era.
home_why_stat1_body: The question-asking surface has collapsed as AI tools answer first, down from more than 200,000 questions a month at the 2014 peak. Documentation is still the number-one way developers learn; they just reach it through AI now, where accuracy and attribution aren't guaranteed.
home_why_stat2_body: Most maintainers writing the knowledge are unpaid. Their work gets scraped by AI without credit. The feedback loop that motivated good writing has been quietly eaten by training pipelines.
home_why_stat3_body: Structured for AI grounding, verifiable for human trust. AGPL-licensed and free for everyone, forever. The intended funding model is sponsorship from infrastructure companies whose tooling benefits from being part of the substrate.
home_status_body: Scaffolding the platform now, in public, under AGPL. Code, design decisions, and roadmap are all visible. Follow along, file issues, or write the first guides.
error_404_body: Sadly, not what you were looking for. This path doesn't exist yet. docolin is pre-alpha, so plenty of routes from the nav are still placeholders.
error_404_pangolin_alt: The docolin pangolin, sitting and waiting
error_5xx_body: An error happened while loading this page. Try refreshing, or head back home. If it keeps happening, file an issue on GitHub.
error_generic_body: We couldn't load this page. Head back home or try again.
onboarding_subtitle: Your handle is how everyone (and every AI) finds your work. You can't change it later. Support can adjust it in rare cases.
onboarding_handle_hint_idle: Lowercase letters, numbers, and dashes. 3 to 30 characters.
onboarding_handle_status_invalid_format: Only lowercase letters, numbers, and single dashes. Can't start or end with a dash.
onboarding_handle_status_v_numeric: Looks like a version number. Pick something else.
onboarding_handle_status_reserved_taxono: Reserved by the kinds taxonomy. Pick another.
onboarding_handle_status_reserved_system: Reserved by the platform. Pick another.
onboarding_handle_status_reserved_preres: Reserved for a real-world entity. If you represent it, you can request a separate org later. For your personal handle, pick another.
onboarding_handle_status_matches_prefix: Starts with a reserved prefix. Pick another.
onboarding_handle_status_matches_suffix: Ends with a reserved suffix. Pick another.
onboarding_displayname_hint: Optional. Shown next to your handle on contributions.
onboarding_error_not_authenticated: Your sign-in session expired. Please sign in again.
onboarding_error_provision_failed: Couldn't create your account. Please try again.
dashboard_load_error: Couldn't load this page. Please refresh and try again.
dashboard_welcome_strip_title: Everything you publish lives in an org.
dashboard_welcome_strip_body: Your personal org is ready. Open it to add your first project.
dashboard_org_projects_empty_body: Projects organize your docos and connect to a source. Git-backed projects sync from a repo; native projects let you write directly on docolin.
dashboard_new_org_card_body: Spin up a separate namespace for a brand, team, or project.
mcp_tokens_intro: Let an AI agent ground its answers in docolin's docs, with attribution in every citation. The endpoint is read-only and public; a personal token only adds your name to the docos you verify.
mcp_tokens_connect_body: Add this as a Streamable HTTP MCP server in your client. For signed verification, send a personal token below as the Bearer credential; without one, the connection still works and reads everything.
mcp_tokens_list_intro: A token signs your agent's verifications to your account, so a doco it confirms working carries your weight instead of an anonymous vote.
mcp_tokens_empty_body: No tokens yet. Create one to verify docos under your account.
mcp_tokens_name_hint: So you can recognize it later. Visible only to you.
mcp_tokens_created_body: Copy it now. For your security, it won't be shown again.
mcp_landing_meta_title: docolin MCP, ground your AI in verified docs
mcp_landing_meta_description: Connect docolin to your AI agent over MCP. It grounds answers in community-verified documentation and cites the author of every doco it uses, instead of guessing from stale training data.
mcp_landing_howto_name: Connect docolin to your AI agent
mcp_landing_hero_title: Your AI reads the same verified docs you do.
mcp_landing_hero_subtitle: And it credits who wrote them. Connect docolin over MCP so your agent grounds its answers in community-verified documentation, with attribution in every citation, instead of guessing from stale training data.
mcp_landing_hero_cta_note: The endpoint is public and read-only. A personal token is optional; it only signs the verifications your agent records.
mcp_landing_demo_title: One question, the right verified answer.
mcp_landing_demo_caption: The same doco a human would open, with the same verification and the same author, surfaced to your agent alongside a citation it has to pass on.
mcp_landing_pillars_title: Two things a base model can't give you.
mcp_landing_pillar_verified_body: Every doco carries a Pango score: a signal from real people and agents confirming it worked on a specific system, with the setup it applies to. Your agent can prefer what's confirmed for the user's machine and flag what isn't.
mcp_landing_pillar_verified_agent: verified by an agent that ran the steps
mcp_landing_pillar_attributed_body: When your agent uses a doco, the citation names the author and links the source. Contributors get credit whenever their work informs an answer, which is what makes the commons worth writing for.
mcp_landing_pillar_attributed_artifact_b: Enable the firewall with a default-deny policy, then allow OpenSSH before you turn it on.
mcp_landing_connect_step1_body: Add docolin as a Streamable HTTP MCP server in your client: Claude Code, Cursor, or any MCP-capable tool.
mcp_landing_connect_step2_body: The endpoint is public and read-only, so your agent can search, browse, fetch, and read discussions right away.
mcp_landing_connect_step3_title: Add a token to sign verifications
mcp_landing_connect_step3_body: Create a personal token and send it as the Bearer credential. Then when your agent confirms a doco worked on a real system, that verification is signed to your account.
mcp_landing_tool_lookup: Fast keyword search for exact terms, commands, and error strings.
mcp_landing_tool_search: Semantic search for conceptual, natural-language questions.
mcp_landing_tool_browse_kind: List docos under a topic path, resolved to the user's setup.
mcp_landing_tool_fetch: Get a doco's full markdown, with the attribution to cite.
mcp_landing_tool_list_discussions: Read the community Q&A, fixes, and caveats on a doco.
mcp_landing_tool_verify: Record whether a doco worked on a real system.
mcp_landing_cta_title: Give your agent docs it can trust.
mcp_landing_cta_body: Connect in a minute. Read for free, and add a token when you want your agent's verifications to count.
inbox_done_empty_body: Mark notifications done to file them here.
admin_claims_search_placeholder: Search by id, slug, or handle
admin_claim_verify_hint: Verify this domain owns the brand for the slug above.
admin_claim_siblings_warning_one: Approving this claim will cancel 1 other pending claim for the same slug.
admin_claim_siblings_warning_many: Approving this claim will cancel {count} other pending claims for the same slug.
admin_claim_approve_body: Creates the org and assigns the requester as admin. Any sibling pending claims for the same slug are cancelled.
admin_claim_approve_notes_hint: Stored on the claim record for audit.
admin_claim_cancel_body: Marks the claim cancelled and notifies the user in their inbox with your reason.
admin_claim_error_already_resolved: This claim has already been resolved.
admin_claim_error_slug_taken: An org already exists with this slug.
admin_claim_error_provision_failed: Failed to create the org. Try again.
admin_claim_error_notes_required: A reason is required when cancelling.
dashboard_pangolin_alt: The docolin pangolin, sitting and waiting
dashboard_project_docos_empty_body_git: This project polls your repo daily by default. Once we've pulled it, your docs show up here.
dashboard_project_docos_empty_webhook_te: Want docos to update the moment you push? Turn on auto-sync in the project settings.
dashboard_project_docos_empty_body_nativ: The native editor is coming soon. You'll create and edit docs directly from here.
dashboard_project_docos_empty_body_synce: Sync finished but no eligible Markdown files were found under the configured subpath.
Every eligible file has a problem (see above). Fix them and they'll appear here on the next sync.
doco_copy_markdown_error: Could not copy the markdown. Please try again.
doco_pango_score_be_first: Be the first to verify this on your setup
doco_stamp_question: Did this work on your setup?
doco_stamp_error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
doco_stamp_signin_nudge: Sign in so your verification counts for more.
doco_inline_copy_failed_toast: Copy failed. Select the text and copy it manually.
doco_inputs_secret_hint: Stays in your browser. Never stored, never sent.
doco_inputs_error_hostname: Hostnames use only letters, digits, dots, and hyphens.
doco_inputs_error_color: Must be a valid CSS color.
doco_inputs_error_select: Must be one of the listed options.
doco_var_unfilled_hint: Click to jump to this input.
doco_deleted_body: The source file for this doco was removed from the upstream repository. The content below reflects the last published version.
doco_discussions_cta_body: Ask questions, share fixes, or talk through edge cases with other readers.
doco_edit_on_github: Edit this page in the source repo
discussion_list_empty_body: Start the first one. Ask a question, flag something out of date, or share a fix.
discussion_list_empty_open_body: Nothing open right now. Start one?
discussion_list_empty_closed_body: Closed and resolved discussions show up here.
discussion_compose_signin: Sign in to start a discussion
discussion_new_subtitle: Ask a question, flag something out of date, or share a fix.
discussion_reply_placeholder: Write a reply. Markdown is supported.
discussion_reply_empty: No replies yet. Be the first to respond.
discussion_error_title_too_long: Title is too long ({max} characters max).
discussion_error_body_too_long: Post is too long ({max} characters max).
discussion_error_forbidden: You don't have permission to do that.
profile_empty_hint: Published docos will show up here.
profile_meta_description_user: {name} on docolin: their docos, discussions, and verifications.
profile_meta_description_org: {name} on docolin: their projects and docos.
preview_verify_note: Verification opens once this doco is published.
preview_error_description: The frontmatter doesn't match docolin's schema, so a sync would reject it. Fix the issues below and it renders.
preview_warning_missing_image: Image not found in your folder
preview_warning_broken_link: Link points to a doco that isn't in your folder
preview_snapshot_hint: Uploaded snapshot: re-upload the folder to see changes.
preview_not_found_body: This preview project isn't on this device. Open its folder again from your projects.
preview_doco_not_found_body: There's no doco at this path in the folder.
preview_permission_body: Your browser needs permission to read this folder again. Re-open it to keep previewing.
preview_reopen_body: To preview these docs, pick the folder again. docolin reads it locally; nothing is uploaded.
preview_overview_subtitle: Render your docs exactly as they'll publish, straight from your folder, before you push.
preview_overview_privacy: Everything happens in your browser. docolin never uploads your files.
preview_desktop_only_body: Previewing a local folder needs File System Access or folder upload, which aren't available on mobile. Open this on a desktop browser.
preview_subpath_body: Choose the folder inside {folder} that holds your docs, or preview the whole folder.
preview_unsupported: This browser can't open a local folder. Try a recent Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari.
preview_open_hint: Not working? Some browsers need File System Access turned on, or use Upload folder.
preview_upload_only_hint: Live reload needs a Chromium browser (Chrome, Edge); here, re-upload to see changes.
preview_empty: No projects yet. Open a folder of docs to preview them.
preview_open_description: Pick the folder your docos live in. docolin renders them right here, exactly as they'll publish.
preview_open_subpath_hint: Leave blank for the whole folder. Any depth works, like packages/app/docs.
preview_open_no_docos: No docos found under that path. Check it and try again.
preview_open_card_body: Pick a local docs folder to render and check before you push.
preview_fs_blocked_notice: This browser blocks live preview, so edits won't auto-refresh. You can still upload a folder to preview it.
preview_no_docos_body: docolin didn't find any docs in {folder}. Tell it which subfolder they're in.
preview_brave_intro: Brave turns off the File System Access API by default. Flip one flag and it works.
preview_brave_step1: Copy this URL and paste it into Brave's address bar.
preview_brave_step1_note: Browsers won't let a site link to an internal page, so paste it yourself.
preview_brave_step2: Set File System Access API to Enabled.
preview_brave_step3: Click Relaunch at the bottom of the page.
preview_brave_step4: Come back here and try again.
dashboard_account_delete_description: Deletes your account: your sign-in, email, name, inbox, and MCP tokens are erased for good. Your handle is retired and shows as "deleted account" from then on.
dashboard_account_delete_content_note: Your published docos, discussions, and verifications stay in the commons, de-attributed as a deleted account. If you want specific guides gone instead, delete those projects first.
dashboard_account_delete_blocked_orgs: You still admin these organizations. Delete them (or hand them over) first:
dashboard_account_delete_blocked_project: Your {count} projects will stop syncing and stay as deleted-account guides. To take any down for good, delete those projects first.
Your project will stop syncing and stay as a deleted-account guide. To take it down for good, delete it first.
dashboard_account_delete_error_blocked: Deletion is blocked; resolve the items listed above first.
dashboard_account_delete_error_upstream: The sign-in provider couldn't be reached. Nothing was deleted; try again.
dashboard_org_settings_delete_descriptio: Deletes the organization. This cannot be undone.
dashboard_org_settings_delete_blocked_pr: This org still has {count} projects. Delete them first; the org can be deleted once it's empty.
This org still has a project. Delete it first; the org can be deleted once it's empty.
dashboard_org_settings_delete_blocked_pe: This is your personal org. It's tied to your account and goes away only with account deletion.
dashboard_project_settings_delete_descri: Takes the project down. Its docos are marked as deleted (their pages show a removed banner) but nothing is destroyed: every doco, discussion, verification, and version is kept. Recreate the same project from the same repo to bring it all back.
dashboard_project_webhook_description: Add a webhook so docolin re-syncs the moment you push, instead of waiting for the periodic poll.
dashboard_project_webhook_secret_warning: Copy this secret now. For security it won't be shown again; regenerate it if you lose it.
dashboard_project_webhook_github_step_1: In your GitHub repo, open Settings → Webhooks → Add webhook.
dashboard_project_webhook_github_step_2: Paste the Payload URL above and set Content type to application/json.
dashboard_project_webhook_github_step_3: Paste the secret into the Secret field.
dashboard_project_webhook_github_step_4: Leave "Just the push event" selected, then click Add webhook.
dashboard_project_webhook_codeberg_step_: In your Codeberg repo, open Settings → Webhooks → Add webhook → Forgejo.
Paste the Payload URL above as the Target URL.
Paste the secret into the Secret field.
Set the trigger to Push Events, then click Add webhook.
dashboard_project_webhook_last_received_: No push received yet. Push to your repo to test it.
dashboard_settings_error_already_member: Already a member of this org.
dashboard_settings_error_admin_immovable: The org admin can't be removed.
dashboard_new_project_title_git: Sync a project from a git repo.
dashboard_new_project_subtitle: Your project slug becomes part of every doc URL. You can't change it later. Support can adjust it in rare cases.
dashboard_new_project_repo_hint: Public GitHub and Codeberg repos only for now.
dashboard_new_project_switch_to_native: Or start a project from scratch (no repo needed)
dashboard_new_project_switch_to_git: Or import from a git repo instead
dashboard_new_project_native_banner_body: You'll write docs directly in docolin. No external repo needed.
dashboard_new_project_slug_status_invali: Only lowercase letters, numbers, and single dashes. Can't start or end with a dash.
dashboard_new_project_slug_status_reserv: Reserved by the platform. Pick another.
dashboard_new_project_slug_status_taken: Already used in this org. Pick another.
dashboard_new_project_displayname_hint: Shown as the project's friendly title. Falls back to the slug if blank.
dashboard_new_project_subpath_hint: Where docs live in the repo, e.g. docs/. Leave blank to sync from the repo root.
dashboard_new_project_error_invalid_url: That doesn't look like a GitHub repo URL.
dashboard_new_project_error_repo_not_fou: We couldn't find that repo. Check it's public and the URL is correct.
dashboard_new_project_error_rate_limited: GitHub rate-limited us. Try again in a moment.
dashboard_new_project_error_private: Private repos aren't supported yet. Make it public or pick a different one.
dashboard_new_project_error_network: Couldn't reach GitHub. Check your connection and try again.
dashboard_new_project_error_provision_fa: Couldn't create the project. Try again.
dashboard_new_org_title: Pick a slug for your new org.
dashboard_new_org_subtitle: Your org's slug becomes part of every URL on docolin. You can't change it later. Support can adjust it in rare cases.
dashboard_new_org_claim_explainer_body: If you represent this brand, you can file a verification claim. We'll match your support email's domain to the requested slug. Your account stays usable on your personal org while we review.
dashboard_new_org_claim_details_hint: Anything that helps verification (your role, the company, supporting links).
dashboard_new_org_claim_filed_body: Send us a verification email from the domain you're claiming. Until we receive it from a matching address, your claim sits idle and we can't review it.
dashboard_new_org_claim_filed_mail_fallb: Or email support@docolin.com manually, quoting the reference id above.
dashboard_new_org_claim_filed_mail_body:
Hi docolin team,

I'd like to claim the slug "{slug}" on docolin.

My reference id is {uid}. This email is from an address at the domain that owns the {slug} brand, so please match the domain and approve.

Quick context (who you are, your role, anything that helps us verify):

{context}

Thanks!
{name}
dashboard_new_org_error_provision_failed: Couldn't create the org. Please try again.
dashboard_new_org_error_claim_failed: Couldn't file the claim. Please try again.
dashboard_new_org_handle_hint_idle: Lowercase letters, numbers, and dashes. 3 to 30 characters.
dashboard_project_sync_errors_subtext: These files weren't published because they have problems. Fix in your repo and they'll appear on the next sync.
dashboard_project_sync_error_code_yaml_p: The YAML frontmatter at the top of the file is malformed.
dashboard_project_sync_error_code_frontm: Frontmatter is missing required fields or has invalid values.
dashboard_project_sync_error_code_handle: One or more author handles don't exist on docolin.
dashboard_project_sync_error_code_asset_: An image is too large to archive.
Couldn't upload an image to docolin storage.
moderation_report_description: Tell us what's wrong. We never share who reported content.
moderation_report_details_placeholder: Add any context that helps us review this.
moderation_report_success_body: Our team will review it and act if needed.
moderation_report_error_details: Add a few details for an "Other" report.
moderation_report_error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
moderation_request_deletion_description: This hides the content immediately and sends it to platform staff for review.
moderation_delete_description: It will be hidden right away. Platform staff review deletions, and you'll be notified of the outcome.
admin_moderation_redact_help: Edit out the offending content and keep the rest. The original is destroyed; this redacted version stays visible.
admin_moderation_version_hint: Doco version takedown isn't wired up yet. Dismiss, or note for follow-up.
admin_home_moderation_desc: Open reports and pending deletion requests.
search_prompt_body: Find a guide by what you're trying to do, your setup, or an exact command.
search_empty_body: Nothing matched your search. Try fewer or different words.
search_palette_prompt: Search guides by name, topic, or command
search_browse_body: Pick a topic, or search by what you're trying to do.
search_error_body: Something went wrong. Try again in a moment.
kind_empty_body: No documentation under {kind} yet. Check back soon.
verify_question: Was the AI able to help you?
verify_context: An AI assistant used the docolin doco "{title}" to help you. docolin would like to know whether it actually worked on your system.
verify_signed_note: You're signed in, so your verification is signed to your account and counts for more.
verify_anon_note: You're anonymous. Sign in for a signed verification that carries more weight.
verify_thanks_signed: Recorded and signed to your account.
verify_already_body: This verification link was already used.
verify_invalid_body: This verification link is invalid or has expired.
